## reference · platform as a product internal platform engineering discipline
* A digital platform is a foundation of self-service APIs, tools, services, knowledge, and support arranged as a compelling internal product.
* Autonomous delivery teams can use the platform to deliver product features at a higher pace with reduced co-ordination.
* Five key pillars of platform capability include:
  + Delivery infrastructure
  + APIs and architectural remediation
  + Self-service data
  + Experimental infrastructure
  + Customer touchpoint technologies
* Backlog coupling occurs when changes have dependencies across multiple teams, resulting in 10-12x slower elapsed time.
* A good platform reduces backlog coupling by providing self-service access, including self-service provisioning, configuration, and management and operation of platform capabilities and assets.
* A superficial private cloud approach, which provides limited self-service capabilities without altering the fundamentals of application and infrastructure management, is not effective in significantly changing the pace of delivery.
* Characteristics of a good platform include:
  + Self-service access
  + Reduced backlog coupling
  + Autonomous delivery teams
  + Reduced co-ordination
* Technology diversification can increase drag, while autonomy can speed time to market and increase innovation.
Sources: https://martinfowler.com/articles/talk-about-platforms.html
